item.title: Iran still controls the Strait of Hormuz, and nothing will ever be the same again
item.description: The war has changed the complexion of the global energy equation, with Iran emerging as the new pivot in the emerging new order

item.title: OECD urges governments to rapidly unwind costly fuel duty cuts
item.description: More than 25 countries have introduced universal measures to shield consumers from high energy prices since the start of the Iran war

item.title: BDO axes 31 partner roles as AI pressure grows and profits fall
item.description: Move comes amid downturn for professional services after hiring spree during pandemic

item.description: New job driving Japanese group’s AI strategy would be in addition to running the UK chip company

item.description: And the US might find them hard to give up

item.title: Investors sought to pull $20bn from private credit funds in first quarter
item.description: Big groups including Apollo, Ares and Blackstone were hit with redemption requests at start of 2026

item.description: The US president has trumpeted a ‘historic’ refinery deal but Reliance Industries is only planning a modest investment

item.title: Shipping stalls as Tehran dictates terms in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Despite US-Iran ceasefire deal fewer tankers passed the crucial waterway than during fiercest days of fighting

item.description: Strategy would echo doomsday trades that earned Pershing Square a big windfall during the pandemic

item.title: UK becomes top destination for US jet fuel after Gulf flow squeeze
item.description: Iran war stokes embrace of American exports as Britain’s aviation sector shifts reliance on Middle East supplies

item.description: ‘Armada’ of Asian tankers heads for American ports as energy shortages spread

item.title: Iran war has exposed the weakness of the dollar
item.description: As Opec members have long understood, it is not a good idea to give users of your product an incentive to find alternatives

item.description: Executives across finance and cyber defence map where Anthropic’s Claude “plug-ins” will — and won’t — change work

item.description: Famed investor gathering becomes ‘access day’ with new event launched in South Korea

item.title: ‘A toxic environment for children’ — inside the beauty ‘cosmeticorexia’ battle
item.description: Campaigners say cosmetics companies and retailers such as Sephora are increasingly targeting buyers under 18

item.title: Wirecard trial shows the risks of Germany’s slow legal process
item.description: Financial crime cases can be vulnerable to long delays and procedural gridlock

item.title: Nike’s comeback may not come all the way back
item.description: Rehashing old hits and leaving shop shelves empty has enabled upstarts to steal a march

item.title: BlackRock’s ambitious India shake-up falls short
item.description: Assets under management at joint venture with Mukesh Ambani have declined since last year’s launch

item.title: Canadians likely to see gas prices drop after U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal, but other cost relief will take longer
item.description: Rising fuel surcharges and higher oil prices have pushed up costs from the pump to grocery aisles

item.description: Group of new accounts made highly specific, well-timed bets, resulting in hundreds of thousands of dollars in profits

item.description: Much higher prices at the pumps are taking a big chunk out of drivers’ earnings, prompting many to consider leaving the indsutry

item.description: Residual risks and ruined infrastructure will keep prices high

item.description: Shell had been accused of violating U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission disclosure rules

item.description: Bureau is assessing whether merger raises barriers for competitors and new market entrants

item.description: Despite slightly higher rates, five-year terms offer little advantage over short-term savings

item.description: Though Bill 149 is well-intentioned, experts say similar attempts to weed out discrimination against immigrant candidates haven’t proven effective
